_comment: AI Readiness Assessment — 72 placeholder questions across 7 categories. Replace prompts with real content; keep id, category, and order fields intact.

questions.prompt: How clearly has your organisation defined AI as a component of its overall business strategy?
To what extent has senior leadership committed budget and resources for AI initiatives?
How well-defined are the business outcomes you expect AI to deliver?
Does your organisation have a roadmap for AI adoption over the next 12–24 months?
How effectively are AI projects aligned with key performance indicators (KPIs)?
To what degree do you evaluate competitive AI adoption in your market sector?
How well-communicated is the AI strategy across all levels of the organisation?
Does leadership regularly review and adjust the AI strategy based on outcomes?
How mature is your process for identifying high-value AI use cases?
To what extent does your organisation measure return on investment from AI projects?
How supportive is your organisational culture towards adopting new AI technologies?
To what degree do employees understand why AI is being introduced?
How effective is your change management approach for AI-related initiatives?
Do teams collaborate across departments on AI projects?
How comfortable are staff with experimenting and learning from AI failures?
To what extent is AI training available to non-technical employees?
How clearly are AI roles and responsibilities defined in the organisation?
Does your organisation have internal AI champions or advocates?
How often does leadership communicate progress on AI initiatives to the wider team?
To what degree is there resistance to AI adoption within the organisation?
How well does your organisation retain AI talent and knowledge?

questions.category: AI Readiness: AI Strategy and Experience
questions.prompt: Has your organisation previously run any AI pilot projects or proof-of-concepts?

questions.category: AI Readiness: AI Strategy and Experience
questions.prompt: How well-documented are learnings from previous AI or automation projects?

questions.category: AI Readiness: AI Strategy and Experience
questions.prompt: To what extent do you leverage Microsoft 365 Copilot or similar AI assistants today?

questions.category: AI Readiness: AI Strategy and Experience
questions.prompt: How mature is your use of workflow automation (e.g., Power Automate, Zapier)?

questions.category: AI Readiness: AI Strategy and Experience
questions.prompt: Does your AI strategy differentiate between quick-win and long-term initiatives?

questions.category: AI Readiness: AI Strategy and Experience
questions.prompt: How effectively do you scale successful AI pilots into production?

questions.category: AI Readiness: AI Strategy and Experience
questions.prompt: To what degree do you use external partners or consultants for AI work?

questions.category: AI Readiness: AI Strategy and Experience
questions.prompt: How well does your organisation understand the difference between AI, ML, and automation?

questions.category: AI Readiness: AI Strategy and Experience
questions.prompt: Do you have a formal process for evaluating and selecting AI tools and vendors?

questions.category: AI Readiness: AI Strategy and Experience
questions.prompt: How much experience does your team have with prompt engineering and AI interaction?
How well-organised is data stored in SharePoint, OneDrive, and Teams?
To what extent is your business data centralised and easy to locate?
How confident are you in the accuracy and consistency of your data?
Do you have documented data classification and labelling standards?
How effectively are duplicate and outdated files managed?
To what degree do you have metadata and tagging practices in place?
How mature is your data lifecycle management (creation, retention, deletion)?
Do you have clear data ownership assigned for key datasets?
How readily available is historical data for analytics and AI training?
To what extent do you monitor data quality on an ongoing basis?
How well-integrated are your data sources across business systems?

questions.category: AI Readiness: AI Governance and Security
questions.prompt: Does your organisation have a formal AI governance framework or policy?

questions.category: AI Readiness: AI Governance and Security
questions.prompt: How well-defined are data access permissions across your Microsoft 365 environment?

questions.category: AI Readiness: AI Governance and Security
questions.prompt: Do you regularly audit who has access to sensitive data and AI tools?

questions.category: AI Readiness: AI Governance and Security
questions.prompt: How effectively do you manage external sharing and guest access?

questions.category: AI Readiness: AI Governance and Security
questions.prompt: To what extent do you use Data Loss Prevention (DLP) policies?

questions.category: AI Readiness: AI Governance and Security
questions.prompt: How well does your organisation comply with GDPR and relevant data regulations?

questions.category: AI Readiness: AI Governance and Security
questions.prompt: Do you have an ethical AI usage policy covering bias, transparency, and accountability?

questions.category: AI Readiness: AI Governance and Security
questions.prompt: How mature is your incident response plan for AI-related security events?

questions.category: AI Readiness: AI Governance and Security
questions.prompt: To what degree are AI outputs reviewed and validated by humans?

questions.category: AI Readiness: AI Governance and Security
questions.prompt: How well-documented are your AI risk assessment procedures?
How up-to-date are your Microsoft 365 licences for AI features (e.g., Copilot licences)?
To what extent does your cloud infrastructure support AI workloads?
How reliable and performant is your network connectivity for cloud-based AI services?
Do you have the compute resources required for AI model training or inference?
How well-integrated is your identity and access management (e.g., Azure AD / Entra ID)?
To what degree do you use API integrations to connect business systems?
How mature is your DevOps or MLOps pipeline for deploying AI solutions?
Do you have monitoring and alerting in place for AI services and automations?
How well does your infrastructure handle scaling AI workloads as demand grows?
To what extent do you have sandbox or staging environments for testing AI features?
Does your organisation track which AI models are in use and their versions?
How well-documented are the inputs, outputs, and limitations of each AI model?
To what extent do you monitor AI model performance and accuracy over time?
How mature is your process for retraining or updating AI models?
Do you have rollback procedures if an AI model produces unacceptable results?
How effectively do you validate AI outputs before they reach end users?
To what extent do you track bias and fairness metrics in your AI models?
How well-defined is your model lifecycle management (build, deploy, monitor, retire)?
Do you have a model registry or catalogue for organisational AI assets?
How confident are you that your deployed models comply with regulatory requirements?
